name: Harvard Business Review

length.note: No word count on the official page. Pitch by email to an editor or via Submittable.

abstract.note: Not specified. The pitch answers 7 questions instead.

extra_front_matter.name: Pitch (7 questions)
extra_front_matter.note: Central message; why useful/counterintuitive; what's new; why senior leaders need it; source of authority; research/examples; experience drawn on.

citation_style: Rigorous citations required, though they may not appear in the finished piece. No named style.

formatting.note: Not specified; pitch/proposal model, not a formatted manuscript.

figures_tables.note: HBR retains final rights on headlines and art.

contents: The pitch itself functions as the proposal (answer the 7 questions).
cover_letter.note: No separate cover letter; the pitch is the proposal.

ai_disclosure.note: Forthrightly share whether and how AI tools were used, with the editor and on the copyright form. Humans remain accountable.

distinctive_rules: Pitch/proposal model, not a formatted-manuscript submission.
Five evaluation qualities: expertise, evidence, originality, usefulness, good writing.
Originality bar: ideas easily replicable by simply asking an LLM are rejected.
Heavy editing/rewriting; HBR retains headline/art and print-vs-digital decisions.
